bacterial_leaf_blight.name: Bacterial Leaf Blight
bacterial_leaf_blight.cause:
- Caused by bacterium Xanthomonas oryzae
- Spreads through rain splash, irrigation water, wind
- Favored by high humidity and warm temperatures (25-34 C)
- Heavy nitrogen fertilizer use increases risk
- Enters through wounds or natural openings
symptoms: Yellowing of leaf tips that spreads downward
Leaves turn pale green -> yellow -> white
Drying and wilting of leaves (burnt appearance)
"Kresek" phase: sudden wilting of young plants
Milky bacterial ooze from cut leaves
bacterial_leaf_blight.treatment:
- Apply copper-based bactericides
- Use antibiotics like streptomycin (where allowed)
- Spray neem-based bio-pesticides
- Drain excess water temporarily
- Apply silicon fertilizers to strengthen plants
bacterial_leaf_blight.prevention:
- Use resistant rice varieties
- Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilizer
- Maintain proper field drainage
- Use clean, certified seeds
- Practice crop rotation
- Avoid overcrowding of plants
- Remove infected plant debris

brown_spot.name: Brown Spot
brown_spot.cause:
- Caused by fungus Bipolaris oryzae
- Common in nutrient-deficient soils and drought conditions
- Spread by wind, rain, and infected seeds
- Often linked to poor soil fertility
symptoms: Small circular to oval brown spots on leaves
Spots have gray center and dark brown margins
Lesions may merge forming large patches
brown_spot.treatment:
- Spray fungicides (e.g., mancozeb, propiconazole)
- Seed treatment with fungicide
- Apply foliar nutrients
- Use organic compost to improve soil health
brown_spot.prevention:
- Use disease-free seeds
- Apply balanced fertilizers (especially potassium)
- Maintain proper irrigation
- Improve soil fertility
- Avoid water stress
- Seed treatment before sowing

healthy.name: Healthy Rice Leaf
symptoms: Balanced tillering (not too sparse or dense)
healthy.treatment:
- Continue proper fertilization schedule
- Maintain water level in field
- Apply micronutrients if needed
- Protect from pests (preventive care)
- Avoid overuse of chemicals
- Follow good farming practices
healthy.prevention:
- Healthy Characteristics
- Growth Indicators
- Plant Health Indicators

leaf_blast.name: Rice Leaf Blast
leaf_blast.cause:
- Caused by fungus Magnaporthe oryzae
- Thrives in cool nights and warm days
- Favored by high humidity
- Spread by airborne spores
- Excess nitrogen and dense planting increase risk
symptoms: Gray or white center with brown margins
leaf_blast.treatment:
- Apply fungicides: Tricyclazole, Carbendazim
- Spray at early stage
- Use biological control agents (e.g., Trichoderma)
leaf_blast.prevention:
- Grow resistant varieties
- Avoid excessive nitrogen use
- Maintain proper spacing
- Ensure good air circulation
- Timely planting

leaf_scald.name: Leaf Scald
leaf_scald.cause:
- Caused by fungus Microdochium oryzae
- Occurs in high humidity and poor nutrient conditions
- Spread by wind and rain
- Often appears in stressed plants
leaf_scald.treatment:
- Apply fungicides like Thiophanate-methyl or Mancozeb
- Remove infected leaves
- Improve plant nutrition
leaf_scald.prevention:
- Maintain proper fertilization
- Improve drainage
- Use resistant varieties
- Avoid plant stress
- Ensure proper spacing

narrow_brown_spot.name: Narrow Brown Spot
narrow_brown_spot.cause:
- Caused by fungus Cercospora oryzae
- Favored by high humidity and warm temperatures
- Spread by wind and rain
- More common in nutrient-poor soils
symptoms: Narrow, linear brown streaks on leaves
narrow_brown_spot.treatment:
- Spray fungicides: Mancozeb, Chlorothalonil
- Apply micronutrients (especially zinc)
- Remove infected plant debris
narrow_brown_spot.prevention:
- Use resistant varieties
- Maintain balanced fertilization
- Avoid overcrowding
- Improve field sanitation
- Use clean seeds
